Title: PM rejects claims linking him to funds allegedly kept in Africa
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim rejected allegations linking him to overseas fund ownership, saying they must be backed by strong evidence.
Commenting on claims linked to a viral video involving an individual known as Albert Tay, Anwar said he would not entertain any baseless allegations or accusations.
"If there is evidence, bring it forward. No one is exempt. If there is no evidence, I will not entertain it," he said when asked to comment on the matter.
Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz had called on the authorities to investigate a viral recording containing serious allegations made by an individual known as Sofia Rini involving alleged misconduct linked to a former Prime Minister's officer.
He said the video contained several key claims, including allegations that money allegedly stolen by the former officer was taken to Africa for safekeeping, and that the action was carried out under the instruction of the prime minister himself.
He was reported as saying that Sofia also claimed the prime minister would flee to Africa or Turkiye if anything were to happen in the country, and alleged that the named former officer possessed wealth amounting to billions of ringgit.
Anwar reiterated that he has a heavy responsibility in governing the country and cannot respond to every comment or allegation circulating on social media without basis.
"I have a lot of work. If there is evidence, bring it forward. If not, it will only become a nest of slander," he said at a meet-and-greet session at IPG Temenggong Ibrahim campus today.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Malaysia negotiating with Iran over stranded tankers
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Malaysia is in talks with Iran to ensure two Malaysian-owned tankers carrying fuel supplies are allowed to proceed after exiting the Strait of Hormuz, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
He said the discussions were initiated following restrictions faced by the vessels despite earlier clearance granted by the Iranian government, including by its president.
"One vessel has already arrived (in Pengerang, Johor), while another is close and entering Malaysian waters. However, two vessels that have already exited (the Strait of Hormuz) are still facing several restrictions.
"So, we are negotiating because approval had in fact already been given," he told reporters after Friday prayers at Masjid Bandar Permas Jaya here today.
Also present were Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi and Pasir Gudang MP Hassan Abdul Karim.
On other vessels reported to be stranded, Anwar said one more ship remains at port in the Strait of Hormuz.
"One vessel is still stranded due to technical damage, which has prevented it from departing for now," he said.
Earlier reports said five Malaysian vessels were stranded in the strait following geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man arrested over fatal stabbing of ex-wife in Setapak
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have detained a man linked to the fatal stabbing of his ex-wife at a condominium in Setapak yesterday.
City police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the incident occurred at Genting Court Condominium at about 2.20pm on April 23, after police received a distress call from the public.
"The caller reported that a Myanmar woman was found unconscious at the scene, believed to have been stabbed by a man.
"A police team was immediately dispatched and successfully arrested a Myanmar male suspect on the same day," he said in a statement.
Investigations revealed that the suspect was the victim's former husband, and the weapon believed to have been used in the attack was seized.
Fadil said early findings also indicated that jealousy was the motive behind the incident.
The suspect was brought before a magistrate today and remanded for seven days until April 30 to assist in investigations under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder.
A post-mortem conducted at the Kuala Lumpur Hospital Forensic Department at 9am confirmed that the cause of death was a stab wound to the chest.
Fadil urged those with information to contact the Wangsa Maju district police headquarters at 03-9289 9222, the Kuala Lumpur police hotline at 03-2115 9999, or investigating officer assistant superintendent of police Baldev Singh at 012-480 6062, or to visit the nearest police station.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt considering monthly allowance for Form 6 students - PM
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The government is considering extending monthly allowances to Form Six students, similar to those currently provided to students in the Matriculation Programme, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today.
He said the proposal is in line with the restructuring of pre-university education, following the transfer of Form Six administration from the Education Ministry to the Higher Education Ministry.
"I agree that allowances for Form Six and matriculation students should be equalised. The policy decision has been made, and we are now working out the implementation mechanism," he said during a friendly session with the community at the Teacher Education Institute (IPG) and the Form Six Centre at the IPG Temenggong Ibrahim Campus today.
Anwar was responding to a question from a Form Six student on why Form Six students do not currently receive allowances like matriculation students, despite both being at the pre-university level.
At present, matriculation students receive the Matriculation Student Living Allowance (BSHP) of RM250 per month.
Anwar said students pursuing pre-university studies, whether in Form Six or matriculation, should enjoy equitable access to facilities and financial support, including scholarships.
He said the government's broader reform of the pre-university system aims to strengthen the status of both pathways.
He had previously announced that the
Higher Education Ministry
would fully oversee the pre-university education system, encompassing both Form Six and matriculation.
The move will formally recognise students in both programmes as part of the higher education ecosystem.
–Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Retiree in fear as sinkhole appears on his property due to drainage failure
Article:
KUANTAN: A broken drainage culvert in Sungai Karang Pantai, here, has become not just a nuisance for a retiree; it has now become a growing worry.
This is as the damaged culvert is believed to have caused poor water flow, subsequently leading to the soil nearby to give way.
Mazlan Muhammad, 71, was left stunned upon realising that the water is now flowing beneath his property, leading to the formation of a sinkhole in a cemented section, which had previously been rented out to a car wash operator for more than 12 years.
He said the car wash operator ceased business in February this year as the site was no longer safe, following the appearance of cracks on the cement floor.
"The damaged culvert has caused underground water movement, and the floor has become hollow. The car wash operator left, fearing that the cement floor would not be able to withstand the weight of vehicles.
"The broken structure along the Beserah main road has caused soil erosion, and there are concerns that the main road might collapse. I am worried as my house is only about 100m from the sinkhole," he told reporters when met today.
Mazlan said there are plans in the future to build a house for his children on the land, but it would be impractical now.
"This drainage system is more than 40 years old and channels water from a nearby housing settlement. It is high time the authorities reroute its flow," he said.
Mazlan said he had reported the matter to the local authorities but was informed that it fell under the jurisdiction of other agencies.
Last week, he lodged a report with Beserah assemblyman Datuk Andansura Rabu.
Meanwhile, Andansura said the damaged structure, along with a broken culvert, had affected the landowner's development plans, including the proposed construction of a house.
"During my visit, I noticed that the poorly maintained drainage system is allowing water to seep into private land. During heavy rain, water overflows into the owner's property as the concrete culvert has collapsed.
"I have notified the district office, the Drainage and Irrigation Department and the Kuantan City Council to study the water flow and find ways to resolve the matter," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MEX crash: Wife of victim discharged, still recovering from injuries
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A 23-year-old Bangladeshi woman who survived a crash that claimed her husband's life has been discharged from hospital but remains unable to walk without assistance.
Grieving Nafisa Tabassum Adiba, who sustained a broken leg in the incident, was discharged at 5.30pm yesterday, her uncle Mohibul Hasan, 50, said.
"She was discharged at 5.30pm yesterday.
"But she needs two people to help her walk," Mohibul said briefly to the New Straits Times when contacted today.
In the suspected drink-driving crash early yesterday on the Maju Expressway (MEX), Nafisa's husband, Muzahid Millad, 22, was killed.
Mohibul, who works in Johor, said Nafisa and he are currently staying at an apartment in the city while arrangements are being made.
The family is engaging lawyers to handle legal matters with the hospital, as well as to facilitate the repatriation of Muzahid's remains to Bangladesh for burial.
The
post-mortem
examination has been completed and the body is currently at the Kuala Lumpur Hospital Forensic Department.
A 31-year-old soldier has been
arrested
, and the case is being investigated for murder under Section 302 of the Penal Code.

From: Zaf

Title: Bus driver jailed, fined RM8,000 for dangerous driving
Article:
ALOR GAJAH: An express bus driver was sentenced to seven months' jail and fined RM8,000 by the Magistrate's Court here today after pleading guilty to dangerous driving last week.
Magistrate Dr Teoh Shu Yee handed down the sentence to
Mohd Tamrin Ibrahim,
53, and ordered it to take effect from the date of his arrest on April 21.
The court also imposed an additional five months' jail if the fine is not paid and disqualified him from holding a driving licence for five years from today.
According to the charge, he drove a bus in a manner dangerous to the public at KM196.1 of the southbound North-South Expressway near the Ayer Keroh interchange at about 3.50am on April 15.
He was charged under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987, which carries a maximum jail term of five years and a fine of between RM5,000 and RM15,000 upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Muhamad Ehsan Nasarudin urged the court to impose a heavier sentence, including a minimum one-year jail term, citing the seriousness of the offence and risks to public safety.
Defence lawyer Kamariah Md Yusof from the National Legal Aid Foundation appealed for leniency, saying her client had dependants and suffered from asthma.
In delivering the sentence, the court stressed the importance of public safety, noting that 15 passengers were on board at the time.
The magistrate said that although no accident, injury or damage occurred, the accused's actions warranted accountability as he had failed to demonstrate safe driving practices.
The case came to light after a one-minute-and-20-second video clip showing the driver operating the bus dangerously went viral on social media.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man ordered to undergo psychiatric evaluation after courtroom outburst
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Chaos erupted at the magistrate's court today when an unemployed man unleashed a torrent of profanities, shouting wildly and insisting that the government was demanding millions of ringgit from him.
Muhammad Adam Abdullah, 39, raged on for nearly five minutes at the dock as court police attempted to restrain him but their efforts only seemed to agitate him further.
Deputy public prosecutor Amritpreet Kaur Randhawa then applied for the accused to be referred for a psychiatric evaluation at Hospital Bahagia Ulu Kinta, Perak.
Magistrate Farah Nabihah Muhammad Dan granted the application and ordered the accused, who is unmarried and homeless, to undergo a one month assessment.
Earlier, Adam had pleaded guilty to two charges of  throwing a copy of the Quran and Surrah Yassin into the canteen of Madrasah Al-Islah in Sentul about 6.15pm on April 22.
He was accused of intentionally wounding the religious feelings of the public, including a 43-year-old lawyer who was present at the scene, as well as engaging in indecent behaviour intended to provoke a breach of peace.
Two mosque committee members pursued and apprehended him before the incident was reported to the Sentul police command centre.
Further checks revealed he had a criminal record involving 13 previous offences, including drug-related ones.
The charges were framed under Section 298 of the Penal Code which carries a maximum one year jail, a fine or both upon conviction; and Section 14 of the Minor Offences Act which provides a maximum RM100 fine.
In a separate charge, he was accused of failing to produce his identity card for verification by police at the same time, date and place.
The offence under Section 25(1)(a) of the National Registration Regulations carries a maximum three-year jail term or a fine not more than RM20,000 or both upon conviction.
He was also charged with self-administering amphetamine, methamphetamine and tetrahydrocannabinol at 4.05pm at the Cheras police headquarters Narcotics Crime Investigation Department on April 23.
The charge under Section 15(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act carries a maximum RM5,000 fine or a jail term not more than two years and police supervision of between two and three years.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: NSTP booth draws crowds with youth-focused activities at Karnival Jom Heboh
Article:
BATU KAWAN: The New Straits Times Press booth at Karnival Jom Heboh here emerged as a vibrant crowd-puller, offering a dynamic mix of educational, creative and entertainment-based activities tailored especially for youths and families.
Anchoring the booth's appeal were a series of engaging competitions, including the "Pertandingan Wartawan Cilik", which gave children a hands-on introduction to the world of journalism, as well as an artificial intelligence-themed challenge that reflected NSTP's forward-looking approach.
Creative expression was further encouraged through a colouring contest and an origami competition, with the latter offering a standout prize — an Art Market Malaysia voucher worth up to RM500 — underscoring NSTP's support for arts and creativity among young Malaysians.
The booth also featured interactive attractions such as a photo booth experience, supported by Dengku Gaming Zone, alongside the "U Klik" photo booth, both of which saw steady queues throughout the event.
A fee of RM20 was charged for the whole experience, which comes with goodies.
Winners of the respective daily competitions stand to walk away with a hamper sponsored by Econsave and a trophy.
Visitors were also drawn to the array of giveaways and rewards.
NSTP will be distributing 300 packs of 1kg Beras Jasmine.
Complementing the line-up were gaming experiences, including PlayStation and virtual games, which resonated strongly with younger visitors and added a modern, immersive dimension to the booth.
NSTP business development and special projects manager Tengku Kaifi Mirza Tuan Cherit said the activities were carefully curated to deliver both value and meaningful engagement.
"We want visitors to leave feeling satisfied with what we offer — where the cost is minimal, but the experience and enjoyment are maximised," he said.
He said the initiative also served as a strategic platform to strengthen NSTP's brand presence among the public.
"This is part of our continuous effort to bring our brand closer to the community. In previous editions, we introduced attractions such as light sensory installations and even a petting zoo.
"This year, we are placing greater emphasis on youth engagement, while introducing them to NSTP's brand in a fun and interactive way," he added.
NSTP also distributed free newspapers as part of its outreach efforts, reinforcing its commitment to keeping the public informed while remaining relevant in an increasingly digital landscape.
The lively atmosphere at the booth reflected NSTP's success in blending entertainment with brand engagement, ensuring visitors walked away with a rewarding and memorable experience at Karnival Jom Heboh.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump seeks 'lasting deal' with Iran as Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended
Article:
DUBAI: Israel and Lebanon extended their ceasefire for three weeks at a meeting at the White House brokered by President Donald Trump, who said he was prepared to wait for "the best deal" to end his conflict with Iran.
Fighting between Israel and Iranian-backed Hizbollah fighters in Lebanon has been one of a number of sticking points to resolving the wider eight-week regional conflict, along with Iran's nuclear ambitions and control of the crucial Strait of Hormuz.
Trump said he was in no rush to reach a peace agreement and wanted it to be "everlasting," while continuing to assert that the US had a clear upper hand in the naval stand-off in the strait.
A day after Iran flaunted its tightened grip over the key shipping corridor, Trump dismissed the threat posed by Iran's "little wise-guy ships" and said he believed Teheran was hamstrung from making a deal because its leadership was in turmoil.
On Thursday, he said the US could knock out in a day any refurbishing of weapons that Iran may have made during a ceasefire in place since April 8. But navigation in the passage remained effectively blocked, and the Iranian capture of two huge cargo ships was a reminder that the US struggles to keep control of the strait and Teheran continued to cause trouble for oil markets and pose major strains to the global economy.
The Philippine government said 15 Filipinos were on the two vessels and believed to be safe and that it expected the ships to be cleared soon.
Oil prices resumed their rise on Friday as the ceasefire remained shaky with the blockade of the strait unresolved. The US dollar was moving closely with oil prices, on track for its first weekly gain in three weeks, with dampened hopes for an immediate easing of tensions leading to increased safe-haven demand.
IRANIAN UNITY
Iran's use of a swarm of small, fast boats to seize the container ships cast doubt on Trump's suggestions that US forces have disabled its naval threats and underscored Teheran's evolving tactics in the strait as it countered US interception of Iran-linked oil tankers and other vessels. Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei on Thursday rejected Trump's claim of disarray in the leadership, describing it as "the enemy's media operations" to maliciously undermine Iranian unity and security.
"Unity will become stronger and more solid, and enemies will become weaker and more humiliated," he said in a post on X, as he remained out of the public eye since taking over from his father, the late Ayatollah Ali Khamenei who was killed by US strikes in the early days of the war that began on Feb 28.
The prolonged conflict has deepened the fissure between the US and Nato, with Trump repeatedly criticising members for failing to support US operations. Washington is now weighing punishing "difficult" countries, such as Spain, according to policy options being reviewed on the bloc, a US official told Reuters.
The options are detailed in a note expressing frustration at some allies' perceived reluctance or refusal to grant the US access, basing and overflight rights for the Iran war, the official said on the condition of anonymity.
Trump said this week he would indefinitely extend what had been a two-week ceasefire with Iran to allow for further peace talks, which have yet to be scheduled.
"Don't rush me," he said when asked how long he was willing to wait for a long-term peace deal. "I want to make the best deal ... I want to have it everlasting."
He ruled out the use of nuclear weapons, telling reporters they were unnecessary because the US had "decimated" Iran with conventional arms.
"No, I wouldn't use it. A nuclear weapon should never be allowed to be used by anybody," Trump said when asked by a reporter at the White House.
DEADLY WEEK
Despite the extension of their ceasefire, fighting continued in southern Lebanon on Thursday as Israeli forces pounded Iranian-backed Hizbollah targets following some of the deadliest days since their earlier deal on April 16 to halt fighting.
The Israeli military said Hizbollah fighters attacked its troops in southern Lebanon on Thursday with rockets and a drone and fired rockets towards northern Israel.
Israeli forces carried out a number of strikes in response, killing three Hizbollah fighters and targeting the group's infrastructure used to launch the attacks, it said.
Israel has sought to make common cause with Lebanon's government over Hizbollah, which was founded by Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps and which Beirut has been seeking to disarm peacefully for the past year.
The group was not present at the ceasefire talks in Washington.
Before the announcement in Washington on Thursday, Israel warned it was ready to restart attacks on Iran and was waiting for a "green light" from the US
Defence Minister Israel Katz said if it did, it would begin by targeting Khamenei and "return Iran to a dark age." --REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan royal dispute to be settled under state law, says PM
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has left the ongoing conflict between Yang di-Pertuan Besar Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir and the four Undang to be resolved in accordance with state law.
Anwar said he is leaving it to the wisdom of the relevant parties to resolve the matter appropriately.
"The (crisis in) Negri Sembilan is calm now. Let it be managed under the Negri Sembilan law first.
"It will take some time; we leave it to the wisdom of (those involved)," he said when asked to comment on the crisis.
The conflict arose when the Undang of the districts exercised their authority to remove Tuanku Muhriz as Yang di-Pertuan Besar, while the Undang of Sungei Ujong was also alleged to have been dismissed.
It was reported that the first sitting of the 15th Negri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly was
postponed
to a date to be announced later.
However, State Assembly Speaker Datuk MK Ibrahim Abd Rahman did not state the reason for the postponement, but hoped the ongoing crisis in Negri Sembilan could be resolved before the next sitting.
He also said the
absence of the four Undang
and Tunku Besar Tampin at the opening ceremony did not affect the proceedings of the official event.
He said the sitting could proceed as usual and Tuanku Muhriz's presence was only to officiate the ceremony and deliver the royal address.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Haze hits 10 Bachok schools as peat fires push API to very unhealthy levels
Article:
BACHOK: Ten schools here have been affected by haze following ongoing peatland fires in Kampung Kuau and surrounding areas, with Air Pollutant Index (API) readings reaching very unhealthy levels.
The affected schools are Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Kandis, Sekolah Kebangsaan (SK) Kandis, SMK Kubang Telaga, SK Kubang Telaga, SK Beris Lalang, SK Bakong, SK Telaga Ara, SMK Agama Tok Bachok (SMKATB), Sekolah Menengah Ugama (A) Darul Iman and SK Seneng.
District Disaster Management Committee chairman Mohd Zaki Yusoff said the Department of Environment (DoE) had deployed a mobile air quality monitoring station at SK Beris Lalang to monitor the impact zone within a 5km radius.
"Technical readings between 11.30pm and 9.30am showed high concentrations of fine particulate matter (PM2.5 and PM10), at between 170 and 200 micrograms per cubic metre, equivalent to API readings of 201 to 300, categorised as very unhealthy.
"However, air quality has since improved and returned to normal levels from 10am daily," he said when contacted.
He said the Bachok District Education Office had issued instructions for schools to implement immediate measures in line with the Haze Management Guidelines issued by the Education Ministry and relevant circulars.
Zaki said the integrated operation to extinguish the peat fire in Kampung Kuau, Gunong, known as Op Padam, had made significant progress, with 97 per cent of the affected area now fully extinguished.
He said 62 of the 64 hectares affected had been brought under control following swift action by the Bachok district police chief, who is serving as disaster operations commander, and the Fire and Rescue zone chief as deputy commander, with support from technical agencies including the DoE, the Department of Irrigation and Drainage and the Minerals and Geoscience Department.
The operation also involves the Land and District office, the district council – Islamic Tourism Town (MDB-BPI), Bachok Civil Defence Force and the People's Volunteer Corps.
"Although the main fire has been extinguished, close monitoring of air quality will continue to mitigate haze impact, particularly in surrounding areas, including educational institutions," he said.
Meanwhile, Kelantan Education Department director Siti Norina Muhamad confirmed that reports on the situation involving the 10 schools had been received.
She said teaching and learning sessions were continuing as usual, with no school closures so far.
SK Beris Lalang Parent-Teacher Association chairman Anuar Salleh said all outdoor activities had been temporarily suspended, with students advised to wear face masks to school.
"We hope school sessions can be delayed to between 9am and 10am, when the haze has eased," he said.
A parent, Wan Mohd Shahlan Wan Zakaria, 40, said the haze, which has persisted for about two weeks, had affected the health of his three children studying at SK Beris Lalang and another at Sekolah Menengah Ugama (A) Darul Iman.
"Some students have begun coughing, experiencing asthma symptoms and eye irritation, forcing them to return home early from school. We hope for swift action by the authorities to fully extinguish the fire and prevent further air pollution and health risks," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: U Business launches ULTRA Business Reserve for enterprise priority connectivity
Article:
U Business has introduced ULTRA Business Reserve, mobile plan to support enterprises with priority connectivity, seamless roaming and built-in security.
The plan reflects the growing demand for reliable and secure mobile solutions, as businesses continue to operate across digital platforms and international markets.
STRONGER PERFORMANCE
The plan's main feature is ULTRA5G Reserve, which provides always-on reserved network capacity. This allows users to maintain stable connectivity during peak usage periods, when networks are typically busy.
Businesses can experience speeds up to four times faster performance during peak hours, which is optimal for video calls, cloud-based work and real-time collaboration.
SEAMLESS CONNECTIVITY ACROSS BORDERS
U Business has also emphasised improving connectivity for business travellers. Through its Premier Roaming , leveraging on Tier-1 network partners to offer coverage & free incoming calls in over 60 destinations.
The service is designed to provide a consistent connection without the need to switch networks or manage multiple roaming plans. This enables professionals to remain connected and productive while travelling.
As work becomes more mobile and digitally driven, the introduction of ULTRA Business Reserve highlights the increasing need for connectivity that is not only fast but also secure and reliable.
BUILT-IN PROTECTION
Security remains a key component of the proposition. ULTRA Business Reserve includes AI-powered Intelligent Security protection against scam calls, fraudulent SMS messages and malicious websites.
This protection is embedded within the network, so businesses do not need to install additional applications or configure their devices. The always-on feature ensures continuous protection.
By combining connectivity, roaming and security into a single solution, U Business aims to support businesses that require consistent performance across different locations.
For more information on ULTRA Business Reserve, visit
U Business's official website
.

Title: Fahmi on two-day working visit to Penang to engage communities
Article:
GEORGE TOWN:  Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil is on a two-day working visit to Penang starting today to engage with local communities through various programmes.
He is scheduled to begin his visit at 4.30pm with a site inspection of the Fibre-to-the-Home project at the Armed Forces Family Housing at Butterworth Air Base.
In the evening, he will attend the Wayang Pacak programme at the MPKK Permatang Pasir cabin in Permatang Pauh.
Tomorrow, Fahmi will present contributions from the Tabung Kasih@Hawana fund to RTM Penang assistant engineer Razif Saidin at Jalan Bagan Belat, Pekan Baru, Penaga.
Razif suffered a C4 nerve injury after being involved in a road accident in April 2025.
The Unity Government spokesman is also scheduled to attend the Jiwa Madani programme at Dewan Tok Mat Yahoo in Kepala Batas for a briefing session and the presentation of appointment letters to the Penang Madani Community Leaders.
He will wind up his visit by attending the opening ceremony of the National Information Dissemination Centre (Nadi) in Kubang Semang together with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, which will also witness the recognition of the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission Nadi as an International Telecommunication Union Digital Transformation Centre.
– Bernama
TAGS: Fahmi Fadzil, working visit, Penang, Tabung Kasih@HAWANA, NADI

Title: Female interpreter, two men cleared of conspiring to murder her husband
Article:
KLANG: The High Court here today acquitted and discharged a freelance female interpreter and two men from charges of conspiring to murder her husband four years ago.
Judge Norliza Othman acquitted Leong Eng Eng, 41, Lim Yon Hay, 40, and Mok Kum Loon, 41, after the court found that the prosecution failed to establish a prima facie case.
The court also allowed an application to withdraw the same charge against another man, Yon Hay's brother Lim Yon Loon, who died last year from tuberculosis.
According to the charge, all the accused conspired to murder Chow Tiam Hee, 41, in a house in Taman Anika, Sungai Udang, here, between 1.20am and 1.40am on Sept 15, 2022.
In her ruling, Norliza said the prosecution failed to prove that all the accused had conspired to murder in the incident, with no evidence from any prosecution witness confirming their conspiracy either directly or circumstantially.
"Leong was the deceased's wife at the time of the incident… to prove she murdered him by inflicting fatal injuries, the prosecution needed more than just bloodstains on her clothes and trousers.
"The prosecution's CCTV footage was rather blurry, except for Leong, the only woman in it, and showed nothing suspicious since she was at her own home," she said.
Additionally, she said, the prosecution's CCTV footage was inadmissible because they failed to produce a certificate under Section 90A of the Evidence Act 1950 to counter the defence's claim that it was a computer-generated document.
Norliza said the prosecution only submitted a memory card seized by investigating officers, leaving no comparison with the original device to verify its authenticity.
"The prosecution produced RM2,600 seized from (Yon Hay), claiming it was payment money, but provided no evidence to back that up.
"From another angle, it is a weakness of the investigation by the investigating officers when there was no important recording, namely the struggle, to show that the suspects who fought with the deceased and injured him were all the accused," she said.
Consequently, she said investigators failed to probe the connections between all the accused, including who was at the house that night and their links to Leong.
"I rule that at the end of the prosecution's case, they failed to establish a prima facie case against any of you on the murder charge under Section 302 of the Penal Code, read with Sections 34 and 109.
"You three are therefore acquitted and discharged, with no need to defend yourselves on these charges," she said.
The prosecution called 22 witnesses over the course of the trial, which began in September 2023.
Deputy public prosecutor Nurul Ashiqin Zulkifli led the prosecution, while Datuk Mohd Daud Leong Abdullah represented Leong, with Tan Choon Hong acting for Yon Hay and V. Ayasamy for Mok.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: King, Johor Regent receive PM Anwar in audience
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, and Johor Regent Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim received an audience with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today.
The meeting, held earlier today, was shared by Tunku Ismail in a post on his official Facebook page.
"His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia and His Royal Highness Tunku Ismail, the Regent of Johor, received in audience the Prime Minister of Malaysia, Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today, April 24, 2026," the post said.
The post was accompanied by several photos of the meeting.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China calls US accusations of AI technology theft 'baseless'
Article:
BEIJING: Beijing on Friday refuted accusations made by the White House that Chinese entities were involved in "industrial-scale" efforts to steal US artificial intelligence technology.
The charges were made Thursday in a post on X by White House science and technology chief Michael Kratsios, who also warned that Washington would "be taking action to protect American innovation".
Asked about the matter at a regular news conference in Beijing on Friday, Chinese foreign ministry spokesman Guo Jiakun said: "The US claims are entirely baseless."
"They are a slanderous smear against the achievements of China's artificial intelligence industry. China firmly opposes this," he said.
"We urge the US side to respect the facts, abandon prejudice, cease technological containment and suppression of China and do more to facilitate technological exchange and cooperation between the two countries," Guo added.
The rebuttal comes hours after Chinese startup DeepSeek released a new and long-awaited AI model, more than a year after it stunned the world with a low-cost reasoning model that matched the capabilities of US rivals.
The post by Kratsios accused "foreign entities, primarily in China" of "distillation", a common practice within AI development, often used by companies to create cheaper, smaller versions of their own models.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM approves RM300,000 allocation to rebuild Pulau Gaya bridge
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The government has approved a special allocation of RM300,000 to rebuild a pedestrian bridge at Kampung Selamat Lok Urai, Pulau Gaya, Sabah, which collapsed on March 22, according to the Implementation Coordination Unit, Prime Minister's Department (ICU PMD).
It said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim approved the allocation following the incident, which saw several residents fall into the sea while visiting relatives during Aidilfitri.
"This swift decision reflects the government's concern for the difficulties faced by residents, as well as the priority given to public safety," it said in a statement today.
It said the allocation will be fully utilised to rebuild a stronger and safer pedestrian bridge, replacing the old and dilapidated structure, which posed safety risks.
"The project will be monitored by ICU PMD Sabah to ensure its prompt completion for the convenience of residents," it said, adding that the initiative is in line with the Malaysia Madani aspiration, which emphasises compassion and well-being, as well as inclusive infrastructure development.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Unisem slips into the red in Q1 on margin pressure
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Unisem (M) Bhd slipped into the red in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026, mainly due to weaker gross profit margins amid higher production costs.
The group posted a net loss of RM13.35 million, compared with a net profit of RM6 million in the same quarter last year, according to a filing with Bursa Malaysia.
Revenue, however, rose 9.7 per cent to RM464.75 million from RM423.62 million, driven mainly by higher sales volumes across its key operating segments.
No dividend was declared for the quarter.
The semiconductor assembly and test services provider said it continues to operate in a challenging environment marked by rising cost pressures and foreign exchange volatility, including a weaker US dollar.
"Nevertheless, the directors remain optimistic on the company's prospects, supported by demand arising from artificial intelligence-related applications and continued investment in data centre infrastructure," it said.
Unisem said it will continue to prioritise cost optimisation and operational improvements to enhance efficiency and ease margin pressures, while remaining vigilant to external risks and adapting to changing market conditions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Calls for justice grow online after Bangladeshi gamer dies in crash
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Calls for justice have intensified online following the death of a Bangladeshi national in a crash on the Maju Expressway, with many users demanding accountability over the incident.
Muzahid Millad, 22, widely known as gaming
content creator
"Advance Gaming", was killed after the vehicle he was travelling in with his wife was struck while they were en route from Kuala Lumpur International Airport.
His wife,
Nafisa
Tabassum Adiba, 23, sustained injuries and is receiving treatment at Hospital Kuala Lumpur.
The crash is believed to have involved a
soldier
under the influence of alcohol, prompting an outpouring of reactions online, particularly among Bangladeshi users.
Among them, a user identified as @RidoyRidoy-r3w wrote: "Assalamualaikum… how did the Advance Gaming die? The people of Bangladesh want to know… we heard the driver was under the influence of alcohol."
Another user, @NURNOBI-k6y5n, posted: "We want justice, Bangladeshi people."
The incident has also prompted broader discussions on road safety and drink-driving, with users calling for firm action against offenders.
At the same time, Malaysian users also weighed in, with some calling for more responsible discussion and urging that the tragedy not be framed along racial lines.
User @stevenanda commented: "Sad situation and to add insult to injury, people are commenting 'Type this, Type that'. What have we become?"
Another user, @dylankartwheel, wrote: "Please stop making DUI issue about a race thing. The truth is that, it can happen to any of us. Do you think the driver will look at the victim's face before they crash into them? Stop drink & drive."
Police have opened a
murder
investigation into a military personnel arrested on suspicion of drink-driving following a fatal crash on the Maju Expressway in Kuala Lumpur early yesterday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Suhakam calls for end to indefinite detention of children
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Office of the Children's Commissioner of the Human Rights Commission of Malaysia (Suhakam) has urged the government to amend Section 97 of the Child Act by abolishing 'detention at the pleasure of the Ruler'.
It called for the provision to be replaced with a sentencing framework that carries a definite duration, is transparent, and is subject to review.
The call was made in response to ongoing concerns over the indefinite detention of children, which is seen as inconsistent with the fundamental principles of children's rights, including legal certainty, proportionality, rehabilitation, and reintegration.
According to the statement, the absence of a clear sentencing period and a judicially reviewable mechanism leaves children facing prolonged uncertainty over their fate within the justice system.
"The need for legal reform is most urgent. From a children's rights perspective, any law governing children in conflict with the law must meet at least four minimum requirements.
"First, children must know the legal consequences of the sentence imposed. Second, detention must be exceptional and for the shortest appropriate period.
"Third, the law must provide a genuine and reviewable opportunity for release based on rehabilitation and maturity. Fourth, the process must be sufficiently transparent to prevent arbitrary or disproportionate deprivation of liberty.
"The Children's Commissioner hereby urges the government to amend Section 97 of the Child Act by abolishing 'detention at pleasure' for children convicted of capital offences and replacing it with a sentencing and review framework that carries a definite duration, is transparent, reviewable, and fully consistent with constitutional guarantees and Malaysia's obligations under the United Nations Convention on the Rights of the Child (UNCRC).
"A child-centred justice system should prioritise rehabilitation over punishment and ensure that no child is left in prolonged legal uncertainty," the statement said today.
Earlier, seven inmates convicted of serious offences committed when they were minors
failed in their applications to obtain leave
to review earlier court decisions that had imposed indefinite detention at the pleasure of the Ruler.
A five-member Federal Court panel chaired by Chief Justice Datuk Seri Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh ruled that the applicants had failed to meet the prerequisites for obtaining leave to review those decisions under Rule 137 of the Federal Court Rules 1995.
Also sitting were Chief Judge of Sabah and Sarawak Datuk Azizah Nawawi and Federal Court judges Datuk Rhodzariah Bujang, Datuk Che Mohd Ruzima Ghazali, and Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah.
Sequerah, in delivering the court's unanimous decision, said there were no special circumstances that warranted the granting of such leave.
Meanwhile, the commissioner described the continued indefinite detention of children as concerning, while respecting the Federal Court's decision upholding the validity of the provision.
While the court ruled that Section 97(2) remained valid, a distinction between adult and juvenile offenders was permissible, and "at pleasure" detention could not be determined by the court as it fell within the prerogative power of the King.
"Children should not be left without clarity on the basis, duration, and review of their detention," said the commissioner.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man killed when SUV plunged into river was headed to visit grandfather's grave
Article:
TAPAH: A man's wish to pay respects to his late grandfather remained unfulfilled when he was killed after the sport utility vehicle he was driving skidded and plunged into a river at Batu 22, Jalan Tapah-Cameron Highlands, last night.
In the 8.44pm incident, the victim, Zamri Hashim, 44, died at the scene after becoming trapped, while his wife and four children sustained injuries.
A cousin of the victim, Salabiah Saharudin, 59, said Zamri had earlier been on his way to Cameron Highlands to visit his grandfather's grave.
"A relative told us he wanted to go and pay his respects to his grandfather who had passed away in Cameron Highlands.
"Even though the burial had already been completed, he insisted on going," she told
Harian Metro
when met at Hospital Tapah.
Salabiah said the last time she met Zamri was during a family wedding, where he mentioned that he was not in good health and had a heart condition.
"He was a kind person. We spoke as usual and caught up. At that time, he said he was feeling okay," she added.
District police chief Superintendent Abdul Malik Hasim said preliminary investigations found that the vehicle was travelling from Kuala Lumpur towards Tanah Rata, Cameron Highlands.
He said the SUV was believed to have gone out of control when reaching a bend in the road, veered off to the left and plunged into a ravine before ending up in a river.
"No collision with other vehicles was involved. At the time of the incident, a lorry coming from the opposite direction was present but not involved in the crash," he said in a statement.
He added that the victim was confirmed dead at the scene by medical officers, while all injured family members were sent to hospital for treatment.
The case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Individual questioned over fake diesel export allegation, says MCMC
Article:
CYBERJAYA: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has recorded the statement of an individual suspected of spreading false claims that Malaysia supplied diesel fuel to the Philippines.
The individual, accompanied by a lawyer, appeared at the MCMC headquarters today to assist investigations into the viral allegations.
In a statement, the commission said it had also seized a mobile phone and a SIM card believed to have been used to upload the content.
"The seizure was carried out under Section 248 of the Communications and Multimedia Act (CMA)," it said.
The case is being investigated under Section 233 of the CMA for improper use of network facilities or services.
If convicted, the offender faces a maximum fine of RM500,000, a prison term of up to two years, or both.
MCMC stressed that it took seriously the misuse of social media and digital platforms to spread false information that could mislead the public.
"Strict enforcement action will be taken against any party that violates the law and jeopardises public order," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 114,000 new jobs expected from digital investments
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The upcoming BICSI Southeast Asia Malaysia 2026 Conference and Exhibition is set to be a major catalyst in empowering the nation's technology and digital sectors, particularly in human resource management.
Human Resources Ministry secretary-general Datuk Azman Mohd Yusof said the event, scheduled for July 2026, will further cement Malaysia's position as a regional leader in the digital infrastructure ecosystem.
He said the country's ICT sector continues to show robust growth, contributing RM451.3 billion, or 23.4 per cent, to the national economy.
"Based on the Malaysia Digital Economy Report 2025 by the Department of Statistics Malaysia, this growth has provided over 1.25 million job opportunities between 2022 and 2025.
"Furthermore, approved digital investments exceeding RM342 billion during the 2022–2025 period are expected to create over 114,000 new job opportunities, the majority of which are in high-skilled fields," he said in a statement following the BICSI Exhibition pre-launch here today.
The pre-launch event brought together key stakeholders, including government agencies and industry players such as the Malaysia Digital Economy Corporation, Malaysia Technology Development Corporation, Invest Selangor, Selangor Information Technology and Digital Economy Corporation, and Cyberview.
Azman said the Human Resources Ministry's priority is to develop a future-ready workforce.
The focus, he said, is not merely on increasing headcount but ensuring Malaysians are equipped with the right skills, certifications, and exposure to navigate an increasingly complex digital landscape.
"The Human Resources Ministry emphasises a holistic approach to talent development, which includes strengthening industry standards and expanding access to international certifications.
"Adopting internationally recognised certifications, such as BICSI, alongside structured training pathways, will certainly elevate industry standards while ensuring talent quality remains consistent and aligned with evolving technological needs," he added.
He said closer collaboration between industry players, training institutions, and policymakers must be strengthened to address skill mismatches and support workforce mobility.
Also present was BICSI Southeast Asia executive chairman Dr Garies Chong.
He said BICSI Southeast Asia remains committed to supporting the industry's progress through regional collaborative efforts.
"Our efforts are focused on raising industry standards, developing high-skilled talent, and strengthening regional cooperation, which are the foundations of a resilient and future-ready ICT ecosystem," he said.
KW: Malaysian News, NST, BICSI, Southeast Asia, 2026, Digital Economy, Human Resources Ministry, ICT Sector, 114,000
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Grief spreads online after Bangladeshi YouTuber Muzahid Millad dies in crash
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Bangladeshi online gaming community is mourning the death of Muzahid Millad, a Bangladeshi tourist who was killed in a suspected drink-driving crash on the Maju Expressway (MEX) early yesterday.
Tributes have been posted across social media platforms, with fellow content creators and online communities expressing grief over the loss of the 22-year-old gaming content creator.
Muzahid's YouTube channel, Advance Gaming, has more than 777,000 subscribers.
A fellow gaming YouTube account, "Gamer With Proverb", uploaded a video titled "End of Mujahid Bhai (Advanced Gaming)", expressing condolences and sadness over Muzahid's death.
Another YouTube user, "I'm Shojib", also shared a video titled "RIP Mujahid", while the Facebook page "Bangladeshi Football Ultras", which has more than 458,000 followers, posted messages mourning his death.
Comments on these posts reflected shock and sorrow within the community.
A user identified as "@GAMING_MINHAJ_YT" on YouTube wrote: "He was a most famous content creator in our Bangladesh… Please justice for Mujahid."
"He was one of the most popular influencers in Bangladesh. We demand justice for Muzahid," another user, "@meheraj-w5g", said.
Meanwhile, "@Trader-o1r" commented: "Justice for our legend Mujahid."
Many of the messages included calls for justice, reflecting the strong emotional response from followers and fans.
Muzahid was widely followed within the community, with his death prompting an outpouring of tributes across digital platforms.
Muzahid's wife, Nafisa Tabassum Adiba, 23, was injured and is receiving treatment at Kuala Lumpur Hospital.
The couple, from Habiganj, a district in northeastern Bangladesh, had arrived in Malaysia for a holiday and were planning to spend a week in Kuala Lumpur.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Scott's marks Children's Day with 'AnakSiapaNi' health campaign
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Scott's marked Children's Day 2026 with its #AnakSiapaNi campaign celebrating children's achievements while promoting health, immunity and holistic development through family engagement.
Led by healthcare company Haleon, the campaign centres on "proud parenting moments" — everyday instances when children show confidence, resilience and curiosity — while encouraging conversations around nutrition and wellbeing.
Haleon general manager for Malaysia, Singapore and Brunei, Neelesh Suryavanshee, said the initiative reflects efforts to support children's holistic development.
"In those formative years, children's immune systems are still developing and need all the support they can get.
Pre-schoolers can fall ill as much as eight to 12 times a year," he said.
He said nutritional gaps remain a concern among children, particularly in essential nutrients such as Vitamin C, underscoring the need for parents to prioritise healthy daily routines.
Neelesh said the campaign takes inspiration from a familiar Malaysian expression often used when a child stands out for their confidence or achievements in everyday moments.
"Through this campaign, we celebrate those proud moments while reminding parents about the importance of building immunity every single day. When children are healthy, they are free to explore, learn and enjoy being themselves," he said.
He said the #AnakSiapaNi campaign was designed to connect with parents in a relatable and meaningful way, using everyday family moments to reinforce messages around children's wellbeing and immunity support.
Beyond awareness efforts, the initiative also includes a community outreach component in collaboration with Watsons and House of Love, involving the distribution of around 1,000 units of Scott's products together with essential items to support underserved children for up to six months.
"We believe every child deserves the opportunity to grow up healthy, strong and confident. Building immunity is a shared responsibility that creates lasting impact," Neelesh said.
Today's Children's Day launch at Sunway Velocity Mall featured speeches, a campaign video, children's performances, interactive games and photo booth activities.
The event also marked the start of a three-day #AnakSiapaNi roadshow from April 24 to 26 at the mall, extending the campaign through family-friendly engagement activities.
Parents attending the launch welcomed initiatives that combine celebration with awareness on children's wellbeing.
Kim, a mother attending the event, said maintaining children's immunity is an important part of parenting.
"As a parent, it's my responsibility to take care of their health and immunity. Scott's has been helping me and my children consume it every day since last year," she said.
Another parent, Norhafiza, 34, said such initiatives help broaden conversations around children's needs beyond academics.
"As a mother, I think this kind of event gives parents awareness about what's important for their overall development, because Scott's is definitely one of the things we can add into our children's lives," she said.
The campaign forms part of this year's Children's Day celebration, combining recognition of children's everyday milestones with community engagement and family participation, while reinforcing the role of parents in supporting children's health and confidence.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DBKL shuts beauty salon, raids two snooker outlets in Cheras [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has shut down a beauty premises and seized equipment from two snooker outlets during a raid on unlicensed businesses in Taman Shamelin Perkasa, Cheras, on Wednesday.
In a statement today, DBKL said four premises were inspected under Operasi Tindakan Khas Premis Berisiko (Pusat Kecantikan/Snuker) Siri 5/2026.
It said the premises were found to be operating without valid licences and in breach of licensing conditions and standard operating procedures set by the authorities.
"DBKL ordered the immediate closure of a beauty premises, seized various equipment from two entertainment outlets (snooker centres), and issued seven compound notices for multiple offences," it said.
The offences included operating without an entertainment licence, employing foreign workers as premises assistants, and failure by operators to undergo mandatory health screenings.
DBKL said the actions were taken under the Trade, Business and Industrial Licensing By-Laws (WPKL) 2016, the Entertainment Rules (Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur) 1993, and the Entertainment Act (Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur) 1992.
It said the seized items were taken to its confiscation store for further action.
DBKL added that enforcement and monitoring efforts would continue, and urged the public to channel complaints through its official portal.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Housing approvals slump 22pct in February, weighs on Leading Index
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The number of housing units approved fell 22.2 per cent in February, weighing on Malaysia's leading economic indicators even as overall growth signals remained modestly positive, the Statistics Department said.
The sharp decline was among the key drags on the Leading Index (LI), which still recorded a 0.5 per cent year-on-year increase to 113.1 points in the month, compared with 112.5 points a year earlier.
The LI is a forward-looking gauge of economic activity over the next four to six months, tracking seven indicators namely money supply, stock prices, imports, housing approvals, manufacturing sales expectations and new business registrations.
"Despite the increase, overall performance was influenced by declines in several components, with five out of seven components recording contractions, notably the number of housing units approved," it said in a statement.
On a month-on-month basis, the LI edged down 0.3 per cent, following declines in real imports of semiconductors and the number of new companies registered, both of which fell 0.6 per cent.
The department said the overall increase in the LI was largely supported by stronger real imports of other basic precious and non-ferrous metals, which rose 18.7 per cent, as well as an 8.4 per cent expansion in real money supply.
"This improvement reflects trade activity and liquidity flows in the domestic economy that remained strong, in line with sustained demand in related sectors," it added.
However, the smoothed long-term trend of the LI remained below the 100-point threshold, signalling that near-term economic growth is expected to stay moderate.
Resilient domestic fundamentals continue to support the outlook, although persistent geopolitical tensions and fluctuations in global commodity and crude oil prices could affect trade activity.
Meanwhile, the Coincident Index (CI), which measures current economic conditions, rose 1.6 per cent year-on-year to 129.4 points in February from 127.4 points previously, supported by broad-based improvements across most components.
On a monthly basis, however, the CI slipped 1.4 per cent, reflecting slower momentum in areas such as real contributions to the Employees Provident Fund, which declined 0.6 per cent amid a more cautious stance among employers.
"The diffusion index for the LI fell to 42.9 per cent, while the CI diffusion index stood at 50.0 per cent, indicating a mixed breadth of expansion across economic indicators," the department said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man rescued after getting trapped in mud near Butterworth jetty
Article:
BUTTERWORTH: Firefighters rescued a man who became trapped in mud near a fishermen's jetty beside a floating seafood restaurant at Ujong Batu, Bagan Dalam, here today.
The Penang Fire and Rescue Department received a distress call at 11.40am and deployed a team from the Butterworth fire and rescue station to the scene.
Assistant operations director John Sagun Francis said the incident involved a man, believed to be in his 50s, who had fallen into a muddy area near the jetty.
"The victim, who is believed to have mental health issues, was found stuck in the mud upon the team's arrival," he said in a statement.
Firefighters, wearing full personal protective equipment (PPE), carried out a safety assessment before conducting the rescue operation using a stretcher.
"The victim was successfully extricated at 12.10pm and handed over to Health Ministry personnel for further medical assessment and treatment," he said.
The man is reported to be in stable condition.
A total of five firefighters and one Light Fire Rescue Tender (LFRT) from the Butterworth station were involved in the operation, assisted by police and Health Ministry personnel.
The operation concluded at 12.39pm without further incident.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police link Bangsar shooting to gang rivalry and territorial disputes
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Long-standing grudges and territorial disputes between organised crime groups are believed to be among the motives behind the shooting incident at Jalan Telawi 2, Bangsar.
Federal police Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said the incident, which occurred about 3am on Wednesday, involved a confrontation between two groups before a shot was fired.
"The gunshot struck the abdomen of a local man, who was subsequently rushed to hospital for further treatment.
"The victim is now in stable condition and is still receiving treatment," he said at a press conference at Bukit Aman today.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt to consider restricting ministerial travel amid global supply crisis, says Anwar
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, April 24 — The government is refining stricter measures to reduce energy consumption amid pressure from the global supply crisis, including considering limiting the movement of Cabinet members and ministries, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
The Prime Minister said the immediate action is critical to ensure Malaysia does not fall into an energy rationing situation, as currently faced by several other countries.
He said a final decision on the matter will be made at a meeting of the National Economic Action Council (MTEN).
“We want to avoid (supply shortage issues). So we need to find ways to reduce energy consumption, such as working from home, as well as other necessary measures.
“We are grateful that, so far, oil supply remains sufficient until the end of June, but diesel supply has begun to face problems. Therefore, several measures will be announced from time to time,” he told reporters after performing Friday prayers at Masjid Bandar Permas Jaya here today.
Elaborating, the Prime Minister said MTEN now meets every two days with ministries and relevant parties to monitor the overall energy supply situation.
He stressed that the current situation no longer allows for any delay in decision-making.
“These measures have reached a critical stage. We can no longer delay as any postponement will affect the economy and the daily lives of the people if a supply shortage occurs,” he said.
When asked whether the global supply crisis would affect the schedule of state elections, the Prime Minister said the matter has not been discussed so far. — Bernama

Title: MetMalaysia: Heavy rain, thunderstorms to hit 10 states until 7pm today
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 24 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit 10 states until 7pm today, according to Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia).
In a statement issued at 4pm, MetMalaysia said the warning covers the entire states of Melaka and Johor. In Kedah, the affected areas are Kulim and Bandar Baharu.
The warning also applies to Penang (Seberang Perai Utara, Seberang Perai Tengah and Seberang Perai Selatan) and Perak (Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Batang Padang and Muallim).
Other states involved are Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Jerantut, Temerloh, Maran, Kuantan, Bera, Pekan and Rompin), Selangor (Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat and Sepang), and Negeri Sembilan (Port Dickson, Kuala Pilah, Rembau, Jempol and Tampin).
Similar conditions are also forecast in Sarawak, involving Sri Aman and Sibu (Sibu and Selangau). In Sabah, the affected areas are expected to be the Interior (Nabawan) and Sandakan (Tongod, Kinabatangan and Sandakan). — Bernama

Title: Johor police arrest suspected drug ring mastermind and girlfriend, seize RM1.95m in contraband
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, April 24 — Johor police have crippled a drug syndicate believed active since early this year after raiding an apartment in Aliff Heights here and seizing drugs and assets worth RM1.95 million.
Johor police chief Ab Rahaman Arsad said the raid at Residensi Mutiara Indah at about 12.30am on Monday led to the arrest of three men and a woman aged between 21 and 24.
"Upon inspection, police found a large quantity of drugs, including over 11.6 kilogrammes of MDMA, 13 packets of ecstasy powder (0.342kg), one packet of ketamine (0.02kg), one packet of syabu (0.11kg), 1,796 Erimin 5 pills, along with repackaging equipment.
"A check of a Volkswagen Golf parked at the residence also led to the discovery of three packets of ketamine, bringing the total seizure to an estimated 12.95kg with a market value of RM1.8 million,” he told a press conference at the Johor Police Headquarters today.
He said preliminary investigations found two suspects were tenants of the unit, while another, aged 24, is believed to be the syndicate mastermind.
The woman arrested is believed to be the mastermind’s girlfriend.
Police believe the syndicate used the gated residence to store and repackage drugs before distributing them to the local market.
Ab Rahaman said police also seized two cars and cash worth RM146,710, bringing the total value of drugs seized and assets confiscated to RM1.95 million.
He said the drugs were sourced from outside Johor and could have supplied about 43,726 addicts.
"The three suspects tested positive for benzodiazepine, while one tested positive for ketamine.
"The case is being investigated under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952, and all four suspects have been remanded for seven days until Sunday (April 26),” he said. — Bernama

Title: Gobind says MSME digital grant payment delays to be resolved by end-April
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, April 24 — Issues involving delayed disbursement of funds under the Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises Digital Grant (MSME) Madani are expected to be resolved by the end of this month, Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo said today.
He said the matter was raised at a recent National Economic Action Council meeting chaired by Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim.
"There was a problem in terms of disbursement of funds, so Bank Simpanan Nasional had actually written to the Ministry of Finance about that some time ago.
"So MOF has said that they will take steps to make sure that the matter is resolved, and we hope that can be done by the end of April,” he told reporters after launching the Microsoft Elevate initiative here today.
Gobind said efforts to address the issue were already under way and that he would continue monitoring the matter closely to ensure it is resolved within the targeted timeframe.
The media previously reported National Tech Association of Malaysia chairman Alex Liew as saying more than 70 per cent of claims under the programme remained unpaid for over six months, far longer than the earlier communicated timeline of one to two weeks.
The MSME Digital Grant Madani offers matching grants of up to 50 per cent, capped at RM5,000, for Malaysian-owned small and medium enterprises to adopt digital tools such as e-invoicing, point-of-sale systems and marketing tools, with applications managed through Bank Simpanan Nasional. — Bernama

Title: Anwar says Negeri Sembilan Undang dispute to be managed under state laws
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 24 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the Negeri Sembilan royal dispute should be managed at the state level.
“Negeri Sembilan is calm. Let them handle the matter with their state laws and wisdom,” he said, according to a report by
The Star
.
Four ruling chiefs — Mubarak, Undang of Jelebu Datuk Maarof Mat Rashad, Undang of Johol Datuk Muhammed Abdullah and Undang of Rembau Datuk Abdul Rahim Yasin — signed a joint declaration on April 19 to depose Yang di‑Pertuan Besar Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, citing misconduct.
They also unanimously named Tunku Panglima of Negeri Sembilan Tunku Nadzaruddin Tuanku Ja’afar as the 12th Yang di‑Pertuan Besar.
The Palace has not issued a public statement, while Mentri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun has questioned the legality of the attempt to remove Tuanku Muhriz.

Title: ‘Bring the evidence’: Anwar dismisses claims of stolen money moved to Africa
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, April 24 — Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim today rubbished allegations that he had ordered a former officer to keep stolen money in Africa, calling on those behind the claims to produce evidence.
Anwar said he would not entertain baseless allegations made in a social media video as he was busy with other matters.
“How do I entertain such allegations? If I have 1,000 comments (asking about the alleged wrongdoing), do I have to answer every one?" he told reporters during a people’s programme at the Temenggong Ibrahim Teachers Education Campus here today.
“They must bring the evidence, if not this will be a breeding ground for lies and so on,” he added.
The Prime Minister was responding to a widely circulated 49-second social media video in which a woman named Sofia Rini Buyong made several allegations involving Anwar and a former officer, including claims of stolen money being moved to Africa and the officer possessing unexplained wealth worth “billions of ringgit”.
No evidence was provided in the video, though several opposition politicians from PAS and Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia have called for an investigation.

Title: MetMalaysia: No tsunami threat after 5.2-magnitude earthquake in Indonesia’s North Maluku Sea
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 24 — A moderate earthquake measuring 5.2 on the Richter scale was detected in the North Maluku Sea, Indonesia, at 12.45pm today.
The Malaysian Meteorological Department said in a statement the earthquake occurred 120km east of Bitung, Indonesia, at a depth of 10km.
However, a preliminary assessment found there was no tsunami threat to Malaysia. — Bernama

Title: Minister: Shah Alam council staffer caught littering from official vehicle will face same punishment as all
Article:
IPOH, April 24 — A Shah Alam City Council staff member seen throwing cigarette butts from a council vehicle in a viral social media video will face disciplinary action and be subjected to a Community Service Order, Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said.
He said no party was above the law and action would be taken against those committing littering offences in public places.
“Not only will a Community Service Order be imposed on (the perpetrators), but disciplinary action must also be taken, as PBT staff, they are required to set a good example and serve as role models.
“I have made it clear many times that, whether you are a Tan Sri, Datuk Seri, employee or any other staff, the law is equal and fair to all,” he told reporters after presenting appointment letters to 160 chairmen of the Village Development and Security Committee of Perak Chinese new villages here today.
The media reported yesterday that the Shah Alam City Council had referred the case to its Integrity Division in accordance with the Shah Alam City Council Officers (Conduct & Discipline) Rules 2023.
A video showing an individual throwing a cigarette butt from a vehicle bearing the council’s logo and name while stopped at a traffic light intersection has gone viral on social media.
The video sparked controversy, with questions raised over whether members of the agency could also face enforcement action, as applied to the public for similar offences.
Meanwhile, Nga said 3,000 individuals have so far been subjected to Community Service Orders for littering offences. — Bernama

Title: Missing Australian hiker found safe on Mount Santubong after overnight rescue operation
Article:
KUCHING, April 24 — An Australian woman reported missing while hiking Mount Santubong yesterday was found safe at 11.59am today after a search and rescue operation launched last night.
The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department said the victim, identified as Belinda Lapier, 43, was believed to have been descending from the peak when she was found by rescuers with only minor injuries.
“On the second day, the SAR operation resumed this morning and the operation commander informed that the victim’s location had been identified in the F7 area (of the SAR operation) at 10.30am. The rescue team managed to communicate by calling out the victim’s name,” it said in a statement today.
The department said efforts to reach the victim were challenging due to extremely steep terrain estimated at a 90-degree gradient, as well as large rocks and fallen trees.
“The victim was finally found at 11.59am in a safe condition, and initial investigations found that she was believed to have strayed from the original route in the F10 area while descending from the mountain,” the statement said.
The search and rescue team is bringing the victim out of the mountainous area to the main road for further action.
Yesterday, the department said it received an emergency call on the incident at 8.20pm and arrived at the location at 9.43pm before commencing the operation. — Bernama

Title: ‘Resolve it this week’, Nga tells Ipoh mayor over old town parking complex land issue
Article:
IPOH, April 24 — Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming has instructed Ipoh Mayor Zamakhshari Hanipah to resolve land issues linked to the proposed parking and commercial complex at Jalan Panglima this week so construction can proceed.
Nga said the issue must be settled by the latest this week to enable the project under the private finance initiative to move forward.
“Currently, we are still waiting for the Ipoh City Council to resolve the land issue as well as the conditions with the concessionaire.
“I have instructed the mayor to resolve it this week. I want the parking shortage problem in old town to be addressed, as it has persisted for more than 20 years,” he told reporters after handing over letters of appointment to 160 chairpersons of Perak Chinese New Village Development and Safety Committees here today.
On April 25 last year, Nga said the five-storey parking complex, to be built on a 0.4-hectare site, would feature 353 parking bays, along with commercial space on the ground floor and rooftop housing a creative cultural centre and food outlets.
He said the project is an initiative by the Ipoh City Council and a private company investing RM15 million in its development, with completion expected within 15 months.
Meanwhile, Nga said today’s ceremony reflected the Madani Government’s continued commitment to strengthening community wellbeing at the grassroots level.
“The appointments are not merely recognition, but a significant responsibility requiring each village head to act as the frontline in conveying government policies, safeguarding public welfare and preserving community harmony,” he said.
He said the ministry remained committed to empowering the development of Chinese new villages nationwide through four key directions: development, beautification, tourism and strengthening local products under the ‘Kampung Good’ initiative.
“To date, a total of 2,750 development projects have been implemented in Chinese new villages nationwide, covering basic infrastructure upgrades, construction of public facilities such as parks and food courts, road repairs and street lighting installations,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Police: RM50,000 debt believed to have led to Johor restaurant shooting that killed three
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, April 24 — Police believe at least six shots were fired in the restaurant shooting at Taman Kota Jaya on Sunday that left three people dead.
Johor police chief Ab Rahaman Arsad said investigations at the scene found six bullet casings.
He said the 71-year-old suspect is believed to have acted over a RM50,000 debt involving one of the victims, a 37-year-old Vietnamese woman.
“The suspect is believed to have lent the money in stages since 2023 to help the woman manage her eatery. However, there was no written agreement between them.
“… the woman is believed to be running the eatery owned by her in-laws, while the other two deceased were regular customers, aged 61 and 63, and were at the scene when the incident occurred,” he told a press conference at the Johor police headquarters here today.
He said investigations also found the suspect held a valid firearm licence issued in Kuantan, Pahang, and not in Johor.
Ab Rahaman said the suspect has been remanded until Sunday to assist investigations under Section 302 of the Penal Code.
“The investigation paper has been submitted to the Johor Public Prosecutor’s Office, and we have been given instructions for the suspect to be charged at the Kota Tinggi Court with three counts of murder,” he said, adding that the suspect is expected to be charged on Monday.
He said the suspect tested negative for drugs and was not under the influence of alcohol at the time of the incident.
In the 1.30pm incident, two men and a foreign woman were found dead after being shot at the restaurant, with the victims believed to have been shot using a shotgun. — Bernama

Title: Man killed, wife and children injured after SUV skids into ravine near Cameron Highlands
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 24 — A man was killed while his wife and four children were injured after their vehicle plunged into a ravine at Batu 21, Jalan Tapah heading towards Cameron Highlands yesterday.
The Tapah Fire and Rescue Station received a distress call at 8.43pm regarding the incident, which involved a Perodua Aruz sport utility vehicle.
According to
Sinar Harian
, the family of six was travelling from Kuala Lumpur to Cameron Highlands to attend a relative’s funeral when the crash occurred.
Perak Fire and Rescue Department operations assistant director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the SUV skidded before falling into a three‑metre‑deep ravine and landing in a shallow river.
“Firefighters arriving at the scene found the vehicle overturned in the river. The driver was trapped in his seat before rescue operations were carried out,” he said in a statement.
Rescuers used special equipment to extricate the 44‑year‑old driver, who was later pronounced dead at the scene by Health Ministry medical officers.
His body was handed over to police for further action.
Sabarodzi said the other five victims — a 48‑year‑old woman, two teenage girls aged 19 and 17, and two boys aged nine and six — sustained injuries.
“All injured victims were pulled out by members of the public before being handed over to Health Ministry personnel for treatment,” he added.
The rescue operation, which ended at 11.02pm, involved 14 firefighters assisted by the Ringlet Fire and Rescue Station, supported by four vehicles including two Fire Rescue Tenders, a Rapid Response Vehicle and an Emergency Medical Rescue Services unit.
Earlier, a dashboard camera video believed to be from another vehicle went viral, showing the SUV skidding while trying to avoid an oncoming lorry on a narrow bend.

Title: Military officer remanded in murder probe over alleged drunk driving crash that killed Bangladeshi man on MEX
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 24 — A military officer suspected of driving under the influence of alcohol in connection with a fatal crash at KM1.9 of the Maju Expressway yesterday has been remanded for five days until April 28 to assist investigations.
Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the remand order was issued by Magistrate Guftan Nor Asyikin Murlikamal following a police application under Section 302 of the Penal Code.
“The remand application was made at 10.45am today at the Dang Wangi District Police Headquarters remand room and was allowed for five days from today until next Tuesday (April 28),” he said in a statement today.
Police received a report on the crash at 5.38am yesterday involving a Perodua Alza used as an e-hailing multi-purpose vehicle and a Ford Fiesta.
Initial investigations found the Ford Fiesta, driven by the 31-year-old military officer, was believed to have entered the opposite lane before colliding with the Perodua Alza driven by a 41-year-old man who was ferrying two passengers from Kuala Lumpur International Airport.
